crie.title: Orphan-Document Sweep
crie.purpose: Safety net for worker crashes (OOM/restart) that skip WF-001's in-workflow HUMAN_REVIEW paths, leaving a document silently in-progress. On schedule, detects documents whose latest processing_history row is PENDING beyond a configurable staleness threshold and remediates them exactly as WF-001 does on a handled failure — using append-only operations only.
crie.reconciliation: Deferred operational safeguard (PROJECT_STATUS §Deferred; DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE §6; RELEASE_CHECKLIST production MUST).
crie.remediation: Decision (approved): APPEND-ONLY. Never modifies an existing processing_history row. Sets repository.documents.status='HUMAN_REVIEW', INSERTs a new terminal FAILED processing_history row, INSERTs the monitoring.alerts row. All performed atomically by monitoring.sweep_orphaned_documents() (migration 0029).
crie.config_driven: No hardcoded cadence/threshold/batch (Principle 7). Reads $vars.CRIE_ORPHAN_SWEEP_CRON, $vars.CRIE_ORPHAN_SWEEP_STALE_MINUTES, $vars.CRIE_ORPHAN_SWEEP_BATCH_LIMIT. The || fallbacks are documented safety defaults; production SHOULD set the vars.
crie.folding_into_wf005: Designed to become a WF-005 sub-workflow with minimal change. It already carries a passthrough executeWorkflowTrigger ('When Called by WF-005') wired into the same Initialize node. To fold in: (1) add an Execute Workflow node in WF-005 pointing at this workflow by ID, (2) optionally deactivate the Schedule Trigger here so WF-005's schedule is the single cadence. No node-graph changes required.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: §157 runs on schedule. Cron is config-driven ($vars.CRIE_ORPHAN_SWEEP_CRON); NO hardcoded cadence (Principle 7).

nodes.name: When Called by WF-005
nodes.notes: Drop-in path for folding into WF-005 as a sub-workflow. inputSource:'passthrough' (LOWERCASE t) per the frozen SW I/O contract. Never fires during standalone scheduled runs; the Schedule Trigger never fires during an executeWorkflow call — so no double-run.

nodes.name: Initialize
nodes.notes: §159 Trigger -> Initialize. Operational parameters are config-driven ($vars); the || fallbacks (30 min, 100) are documented safety defaults, NOT the source of truth (Principle 7).

nodes.name: Sweep Orphaned Documents

parameters.query: SELECT document_id, stuck_stage, minutes_stuck, alert_id FROM monitoring.sweep_orphaned_documents({{ $json.staleMinutes }}, {{ $json.batchLimit }});

nodes.notes: Calls the atomic sweep function (migration 0029). It performs the approved append-only remediation and returns one row per remediated document (empty on a clean run). NOTE ON {{ }}: these are INTENDED n8n interpolations of INTEGER config values (same pattern as WF-001's HUMAN_REVIEW node) — they resolve before the SQL runs. This is NOT a forbidden literal {{token}} passthrough placeholder (e.g. {{ocr}}); no literal braces reach Postgres. alwaysOutputData:true so the Summarize node runs even when nothing was remediated. Bind the CRIE Postgres credential (service_role/owner) on import.

nodes.name: Summarize Run
nodes.notes: Emits a run summary (remediated_count + per-document detail). Real events are already persisted in monitoring.alerts by the sweep function and surfaced by admin.vw_alert_center — this node is the workflow-level heartbeat, kept dependency-free so the sweep stays standalone (no SW-026/SW-028 needed).
